prompt: I need help deciding which engineering skill should handle a mixed API, service, and test change.
Review this new endpoint contract for pagination, error shape, status codes, and backwards compatibility.
Implement the route handler, request validation, response body, and error mapping for a new users endpoint.
Grill this migration and service change like it is about to ship to production.
Add useful documentation for this public helper and update the README with the setup command.
Review this pull request for bugs, regressions, missing tests, and risky behavior changes.
Write a commit message and PR description from the staged changes, including tests and risk.
Before we build this abstraction layer, challenge whether this design is actually necessary.
Design the tables, indexes, constraints, and migration plan for storing organization invitations.
Review this refactor for SOLID, YAGNI, dependency direction, coupling, and abstraction quality.
Create a scoped implementation plan with validation steps for this billing migration.
Production checkout is returning 500s since the last deploy; triage the logs, form hypotheses, and decide whether to rollback or hotfix.
Design structured logs, metrics, and alerts for this payment service so we can debug failures and page on error rate spikes.
/remember I need to send email to product manager at 10 pm tonight
Split this feature into service, API, utility, and test implementation passes.
Review these variable, API field, event, and table names for clarity and domain consistency.
This production issue spans three systems and the symptoms conflict; simplify it before solving.
Draft a respectful but firm Slack reply explaining why we should narrow this feature before committing.
Evaluate this roadmap request from a startup PM and competitor point of view before we build it.
The router missed a recurring skill trigger during real usage; update the skill system safely.
Implement the service-layer workflow, transaction boundary, permissions, and domain errors for invites.
The thread has repeated logs, stale plans, and too much context; keep only what matters for the next edit.
Review these tests for false confidence, weak mocks, flaky behavior, and missing edge cases.
Add focused regression and API tests for this bug fix and report the command you ran.
Write a small parser utility with clear edge-case behavior and table-driven tests.
